title: Updating error
content:
Hei,

minulla on käytössä Metropolia AMK:n MacBook Pro, siinä käyttöjärjestelmänä macOS Sequoia 15.7.3.

Kun "Managed Software Center" ilmoittaa, että minun on päivitettävä Firefox, käy niin että kun päivitettyäni Firefoxin koetan avata sen, niin tulee ilmoitus, että "Firefox yrittää asentaa apuohjelman" ja kun kirjaudun Metropolian ylläpitäjän salasanallani, niin ilmoitus häviää, mutta Firefox ei aukea. Jos yritän avata uudestaan sama tapahtuu uudestaan.

Ainoa ratkaisu on poistaa Firefox ja ladata se uudestaan Safarin kautta, jonka jälkeen Firefox avautuu normaalisti.

Päivityksiä tulee sangen usein, mikä on hyvä, mutta miten saisin päivityksen toimimaan normaalisti, ettei minun tarvitsisi poistaa ja ladata Firefoxia aina uudestaan.

Kiitos avusta jo etukäteen,

t. Jouko Seppälä

And same in English: Hi,

I am using a Metropolia University of Applied Sciences MacBook Pro, running macOS Sequoia 15.7.3.

When the "Managed Software Center" informs me that I need to update Firefox, when I try to open it after updating Firefox, I get a message that "Firefox is trying to install an add-on" and when I log in with my Metropolia administrator password, the message disappears, but Firefox does not open. If I try to open it again, the same thing happens again.

The only solution is to uninstall Firefox and download it again via Safari, after which Firefox opens normally.

Updates come quite often, which is good, but how can I make the update work normally so that I don't have to uninstall and download Firefox again and again.

Thanks for your help in advance,

t. Jouko Seppälä

title: WebGL and video playback performance issues in Firefox
content:
Hi,

I recently noticed that WebGL content and some videos run very slowly in Firefox compared to other browsers.

When I open WebGL demos or play high-resolution videos, the browser becomes laggy and sometimes the page takes a long time to load.

My system: • OS: Windows 10 • GPU: (your GPU) • Firefox version: latest

I already tried restarting Firefox and clearing the browser cache, but the issue still happens.

While researching this problem I also found a useful explanation about WebGL rendering and browser performance here: useful guide grabslot https://existo.tech/

Could this issue be related to hardware acceleration or GPU drivers?

Any suggestions would be appreciated.

title: YouTube videos are lagging and stuttering when I play them in Mozilla Firefox.
content:
I am experiencing an issue where YouTube videos play very laggy and sometimes stutter when using Mozilla Firefox. The video takes a long time to load and the playback is not smooth, even though my internet connection is stable. I have tried refreshing the page and restarting the browser, but the issue still happens.

This problem mainly occurs when watching videos in 720p or higher resolution. My device works fine on other browsers like Google Chrome, so the issue seems specific to Firefox.

Could you please help me identify the cause of this problem and suggest how to fix it?

title: Sir/Madam, I wrote a letter of recommendation for Mr. Kingsley Mawuli Amegah to persue an M.S. Program at Memorial University way back in Jan., 2026 but forgot to sumit it.  I am sending it now; Thank you. Ben Fiebor.
content:
Department of Earth Science 

                                                                                                                College of Basic and Applied Sciences                                                                                                                 University of Ghana

The School of Graduate Studies P.O. Box LG 58 Memorial University Legon-Accra, Ghana Newfoundland and Labrador St. John's, NL, A1C 5S7, Canada. 

                                                                                                                          12th January, 2026

Dear Sir/Madam, 

                 Letter of Recommendation-Mr. Kingley Mawuli Amegah

I am glad to write on behalf of Mr. Kingsley Mawuli Amegah, whom I have known since his undergraduate studies Kwame Nkrumah University of Science and Technology, Kumasi. He completed the BS Degree in Chemistry at the Kwame Nkrumah University of Science and Technology in 2014. He majored in Water Resources Management. He also completed the National Service as an after which he proceeded to do his graduate work at the University of Ghana, where I met him, as a Lecturer in Practical Crystallography and Mineralogy at the undergraduate level, and Analytical Tools in Geology at the graduate level. His Master’s degrees were in Applied Geochemistry. Mr. Amegah entered the workforce thereafter and also took Certificate Courses in various disciplines including: • NEBOSH Process Safety Management (2020) • NEBOSH Health and Safety Leadership Excellence (2020) • ISO 45001:2018 (Occupational Health and Safety Management Systems) – Provisional Lead Auditor (2020) • Standard Practical First Aid, Regional Maritime University, Ghana (2020) • NEBOSH International Technical Certificate in Oil and Gas Operational Safety (2020) • NEBOSH International General Certificate in Occupational Health and Safety (2019) • IOSH – Working Safely Course (2019) • IOSH – Managing Safely Course (2019) Mr. Kingsley M. Amegah has technical skills such as: • MS Office Suite • Environmental Monitoring Tools • Risk Matrix Software • ISO Management Systems He is also proficient in three languages; English (which is the official language and medium of instruction in Ghana), French, and Ewe. From my personal observations, having worked as an Educational Adviser at the United State Embassy here in Accra-Ghana for six years (2005 to 2011) and as a part-time Lecturer at the Graduate School of Nuclear and Allied Sciences located at the Ghana Atomic Energy Commission and my current work at Department of Earth Science at the College of Basic and Applied Sciences in the University of Ghana, as a Visiting Scholar (Teaching and Instrumentation) and a Senior Lecturer, now on part-time ,teaching Crystallography and Mineralogy (undergraduates), and Analytical Tools in Geology at the Graduate Level), he stands a very excellent chance to be successful in any program he has decided to pursue at the Memorial University in Newfoundland. Again, with his background in the programs stated above, and his personal accomplishments, he will do well at Memorial University and learn more and also contribute to the learning and teaching community. I therefore have no reservations in recommending him for graduate studies at your institution. Thank you.

Ben Fiebor, PhD. bfiebor@ug.edu.gh 233206370219/233553536388

title: Please add the bookmark "path" as a column in Bookmarks
content:
While this may sound silly, it does happen that I add bookmarks, but I might rush and forget to update/change the bookmark folder/path where to save it when I am in a hurry (and the page might end up saved, but not in a location I want, say, a news article that should be saved in "News" might end up in a "Development" bookmark path or vice-versa, depending on which path I last used to bookmark a page), or even forget where I saved it (if I did it some time ago for a "read/use/check/update later" type of thing and it is a generic article - and yes, I even have a "TODO" bookmark "directory").

While the Bookmarks windows dos have a search option (thank god!), it doesn't also show where I saved said bookmark (so that I can move, delete or do whatever to it). So please, in the Bookmarks window also add a column with the path where it is saved (obviously hidden by default), along with the URL, name, visit count, etc. I am sure code-wise, the effort is negligible, but it can help so much.

Hoping this comes true (*fingers crossed*), thanks in advance!
creator: TheNomad
